title: calceolaria teucrioides
intro: Calceolaria teucrioides, also known as the kalmia or elfin pocketbook, is a flowering plant species native to the Andes mountains of South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and soothing digestive issues. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping its leaves in hot water, or as a tincture by mixing the plant's extract with a solvent.
